note: Redirect After Login is a core-only module with one settings form (per-role destination map), one config object, one permission, and an event. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) since they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with Redirect After Login, how do I send users of the 'editor' role to /admin/content right after they log in? Name the config object and key.
evals.expected_output: Set the editor entry of the login_redirection map on redirect_after_login.settings to /admin/content (via the settings form at /admin/config/people/redirect or drush cset redirect_after_login.settings login_redirection.editor /admin/content).

evals.prompt: Where is the Redirect After Login configuration form and what is its route/path?
evals.expected_output: Route redirect_after_login.admin_settings at /admin/config/people/redirect (Admin > Configuration > People, menu link 'Set Login Destination').

evals.prompt: If a user has multiple roles, which role's configured destination does Redirect After Login use after login?
evals.expected_output: It uses the last role in the user's getRoles() list — login_redirection[array_reverse($roles)[0]] — falling back to / if that role has no URL set.

evals.prompt: What destination values are accepted by the Redirect After Login settings form for a role? Can I use an external URL?
evals.expected_output: Only internal paths that start with #, ?, or / (or the literal <front>, stored as /), and the path must exist. External URLs are rejected.

evals.prompt: How do I stop Redirect After Login from redirecting when login happens on certain pages?
evals.expected_output: Add paths (one per line, * wildcard supported) to the exclude_urls setting; login on a matching path skips redirection. Password-reset routes are always skipped.

evals.prompt: In a custom Drupal module I want to change or cancel the post-login redirect target that Redirect After Login computes. How?
evals.expected_output: Subscribe to RedirectAfterLoginEvent; call setUrl() to change the target or setRedirectAllowed(FALSE) to cancel (read via getUrl()/isRedirectAllowed()).

evals.prompt: Which permission controls access to the Redirect After Login settings form?
evals.expected_output: The 'administer redirect_after_login settings' permission (restrict access: true) gates the settings form; it is the only permission the module defines.

evals.prompt: How does Redirect After Login differ from the Login Destination module, and when should I pick it?
evals.expected_output: Redirect After Login only maps a destination per role from one form (no conditional rules/contexts); Login Destination is more configurable. Pick Redirect After Login when per-role destinations are all you need.
